_provenance: V1.6 phase-7 voice pack (V1.6_court_rounds.md). Source draft: narrative_revision/phase_7_drafts/V1_6_draft_pass5.md (pass 5 — pass-1 + four §J review passes baked in; arbitrated commit shape).
_scope: judge_district_ch1.json owns the District Court Judge's three rounds of dry-surprise across Beat 12. The states are gated on chapter1.client_meeting_stance ('sympathetic' / 'blunt_procedural' / 'technical') for Round 1 and Round 3, and on chapter1.casebook_judge_state for round-progression. Round 1 reactions also reference chapter1.bonus_evidence_collected ('wojcik_witness_statement' / 'return_to_sender_slip' / 'lease_1962_inheritance_1987') via the stance flag (1:1 mapping per V1.4 — sympathetic→Wójcik, blunt_procedural→slip+countersignature, technical→countersignature only). Round 2 is single-branch (no stance variation in Whimsy's third-clause non-cure preemption argument). Round 3 sympathetic and blunt_procedural variants share remedy-announcement text; technical adds the one-clause acknowledgment that the underlying tenancy is not before the court.
_engine_flags_required: The casebook engine must set chapter1.casebook_judge_state to one of: 'round_1_open', 'round_1_react', 'round_2_open', 'round_2_react', 'round_3_open', 'round_3_remedy'. After each judge state dismisses, the engine should clear or advance the flag. Round_open states fire the bench prompt before the player's argument; round_react / round_3_remedy states fire after.
_address_forms: Bench addresses Cula as 'Counsel' throughout. The judge does not address Halina. The judge does not name the landlord's counsel.

states._comment: Bench prompt at every round transition. Same line at all three rounds.
Round 1 dry-surprise — sympathetic carrier with Wójcik affidavit. Service sufficient on documents alone; affidavit is supplementary.
lines: Counsel. The affidavit is noted. The renumbering is enough for the service question on the documents. The respondent may address the third clause.

states._comment: Round 1 dry-surprise — blunt-procedural carrier with return-to-sender slip + countersignature. Strongest reaction; canonical 'knowledge is not in serious dispute' per pack §B.4.
lines: Counsel. The renumbering, the prior return, and the countersignature are before the court. Knowledge is not in serious dispute. The adult-occupant point is preserved for the third clause.

states._comment: Round 1 dry-surprise — technical carrier with countersignature only (lease + inheritance held for Round 3).
lines: Counsel. The renumbering and the countersignature are before the court. Knowledge is not in serious dispute. The respondent may address the third clause.

states._comment: Round 2 dry-surprise — single-branch (no stance variation). Metaphor noted; third-clause point lands; foreclosure of further service-question submission. 'Lands' tracks §A canonical envelope ('That argument lands, despite the manner of its arrival.').
lines: Counsel. The metaphor is noted. The third-clause point lands. The respondent's adult-occupant submission is answered by Article 135-bis § 2. The court will proceed to remedy.

states._comment: Round 3 remedy announcement — sympathetic carrier. Identical text to blunt_procedural variant. Long-form citation per §A.5 remedy-announcement recommendation.
lines: Counsel. On the motion of the petitioner, the hearing of the eighth is set aside on the ground of invalid service under Article 135-bis § 2 of the Code of Civil Procedure. The matter is rescheduled. The clerk will assign a new hearing date within the statutory window. Notice will be served in the ordinary course.

states._comment: Round 3 remedy announcement — blunt-procedural carrier. Identical text to sympathetic variant.
lines: Counsel. On the motion of the petitioner, the hearing of the eighth is set aside on the ground of invalid service under Article 135-bis § 2 of the Code of Civil Procedure. The matter is rescheduled. The clerk will assign a new hearing date within the statutory window. Notice will be served in the ordinary course.

states._comment: Round 3 remedy announcement — technical carrier. Adds one-clause acknowledgment that underlying tenancy is not before the court (per pack §B.4).
lines: Counsel. On the motion of the petitioner, the hearing of the eighth is set aside on the ground of invalid service under Article 135-bis § 2 of the Code of Civil Procedure. The matter is rescheduled. The clerk will assign a new hearing date within the statutory window. Notice will be served in the ordinary course. The underlying tenancy is not before the court on this motion.
